skill.name: google-analytics-automation
skill.description: Automate Google Analytics 4 reporting and property discovery through Rube MCP, including standard, batch, pivot, funnel, and key event workflows.
skill.summary: Guide GA4 reporting and property workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby shell backticks, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review shows Markdown code spans, front matter, or GA4 metadata guidance. The confirmed risk is use of an external Rube MCP endpoint and Google OAuth to access analytics data. No prompt injection, command execution, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: External MCP Endpoint Requires Network Trust
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The setup directs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This is expected for the skill, but it means analytics workflows depend on a third-party network service and Google OAuth authorization.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is explicitly documented and the skill purpose requires Rube MCP. The risk is limited because no hidden endpoint or credential exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Command, Crypto, and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The many backtick detections are Markdown inline code spans and fenced examples for MCP tool names, not Ruby or shell execution. The weak cryptography alert maps to the YAML description, and the reconnaissance alert maps to GA4 metadata discovery guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation-only Markdown with no Ruby, shell commands, scripts, or executable code. The cited lines contain front matter, MCP tool references, or Google Analytics report parsing guidance.

content.value_statement: Manual GA4 reporting slows marketing and product analysis. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP analytics workflows.
actual_capabilities: Lists accessible Google Analytics accounts and GA4 properties.
Runs standard GA4 reports with dimensions, metrics, filters, sorting, and pagination.
Builds batch report requests for multiple reports on one property.
Guides pivot and funnel report workflows for deeper analysis.
Lists GA4 key events and explains required property identifiers.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Google Analytics OAuth connection.
Does not execute analytics requests without available MCP tools.
Cannot bypass Google Analytics permissions or property access limits.
Today data may be incomplete because GA4 processing can lag.

use_cases.title: Weekly Marketing Report
use_cases.description: Generate GA4 traffic, source, and conversion summaries for recurring marketing reviews.

use_cases.title: Product Funnel Review
use_cases.description: Analyze ordered funnel steps and drop-off rates for a product journey.

use_cases.title: Property Discovery
use_cases.description: Find account and property resource names before building automated analytics workflows.

prompt_templates.title: List Available Properties
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Rube MCP to list my Google Analytics accounts and properties. Show account names, property names, and resource IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Traffic Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GA4 report for the last 30 days with date, session source, sessions, active users, and conversions.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Campaign Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a GA4 report for one property that compares session source and medium by conversions. Check dimension and metric compatibility first.

prompt_templates.title: Build Funnel and Pivot Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a funnel report for checkout steps and a pivot report by device category and country. Use metadata discovery before running each report.

output_examples.output: A concise account and property inventory with display names, full resource IDs, and pagination notes.

output_examples.input: Report sessions and conversions by channel for last month.
output_examples.output: A summarized table of channel performance, sorted by conversions, with notes about metric compatibility and date range used.
A funnel summary showing each step, user counts, drop-off rates, and suggested segments for follow-up analysis.

best_practices: Call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first so tool schemas match the current MCP server.
Resolve account and property names to full GA4 resource IDs before reporting.
Check dimension and metric compatibility before complex or automated reports.
anti_patterns: Do not use numeric property IDs when the tool expects full resource names.
Do not assume today data is complete for operational decisions.
Do not run broad reports without explicit limits or pagination plans.

faq.question: Does this skill require Google Analytics access?
faq.answer: Yes. The user must have a Google Analytics connection through Rube MCP with the needed account and property permissions.

faq.question: Can it create reports for GA4 properties?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides standard, batch, pivot, and funnel reports when the matching MCP tools are available.

faq.question: Does it store analytics data?
faq.answer: No evidence found that the skill stores data. It is a Markdown workflow guide for MCP tool usage.

faq.question: Why does it search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use current parameters.

faq.question: Can it manage key events?
faq.answer: It documents listing key events for a property. It does not document creating or deleting key events.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a network dependency warning. The audit found no executable code or malicious instructions.
